description: Verify the four classical assumptions of simple linear regression — linearity, homoskedasticity, independence, and normality — using residual diagnostics and plots

fields.name: residuals
fields.label: Array of regression residuals e_i = Y_i - Y_hat_i

fields.name: independent_variable
fields.label: Array of X values for plotting residuals against X

four_assumptions.linearity: E[Y|X] is linear in X; residuals should be random, not form a systematic pattern
four_assumptions.homoskedasticity: Var(epsilon_i) = sigma^2, constant across all observations
four_assumptions.independence: Cov(epsilon_i, epsilon_j) = 0 for i != j; observations are independent
four_assumptions.normality: epsilon_i ~ N(0, sigma^2) — needed for inference but relaxable for large n by CLT

diagnostics.residual_vs_x_plot: No pattern expected; curvature => non-linearity; fan shape => heteroskedasticity
diagnostics.residual_vs_fitted_plot: Same — cluster or trend signals misspecification or non-constant variance
diagnostics.qq_plot: Normality check — points should lie on the 45-degree line
diagnostics.durbin_watson: Test for first-order autocorrelation of residuals

violations_and_fixes.non_linearity: Transform variables (log, sqrt); add polynomial term; use non-linear model
violations_and_fixes.heteroskedasticity: Weighted least squares; use White/robust standard errors
violations_and_fixes.autocorrelation: Add lagged regressors; use Newey-West HAC standard errors
violations_and_fixes.non_normality: Increase sample size (CLT); use bootstrap inference; robust regression

small_sample_note.normality_matters_more: For n < 30, non-normality severely distorts t and F inferences
small_sample_note.large_sample_relaxation: For n >= 100 with finite variance, CLT makes t and F robust to non-normality

applications.volatility_clustering: Financial returns exhibit heteroskedasticity — use robust SE or GARCH
applications.autocorrelated_returns: Daily returns may show serial correlation — diagnose with DW or LM test
applications.non_linear_relation: Returns vs size often need log transformation

validation.residual_length_matches: residuals and fitted_values have same length
validation.residuals_numeric: All residual entries are finite numbers

heteroskedasticity_detected.description: Breusch-Pagan or fan-pattern test signals non-constant variance

anti_patterns.rule: Do not interpret OLS standard errors and t-stats when residuals are heteroskedastic
anti_patterns.why: OLS SEs are biased under heteroskedasticity — inflating/deflating t-stats and giving wrong p-values. Use White/HC robust SEs

anti_patterns.rule: Do not ignore autocorrelation in time-series regressions
anti_patterns.why: Correlated residuals understate SEs, inflate t-stats, and produce spuriously significant coefficients. Use Newey-West or add lags

anti_patterns.rule: Do not check normality only via a histogram on small n
anti_patterns.why: Visual checks are unreliable for n < 30; use QQ-plots AND a formal test (Shapiro-Wilk / Jarque-Bera)

anti_patterns.rule: Do not assume passing a single diagnostic guarantees model validity
anti_patterns.why: Each test has limited power; multiple violations can partially cancel in a single test. Run the full diagnostic suite

residual_plot_interpretations.wave_pattern: Autocorrelation — add lags or use HAC SEs
residual_plot_interpretations.clustered_groups: Missing regime indicator — add dummy variable
